stories.headline: Federal Reserve signals unexpected rate pause amid banking stress
stories.body: The Federal Reserve indicated it may hold rates steady following regional banking turmoil, with Chair Powell noting the committee needs greater confidence inflation is sustainably moving toward 2% before easing policy.

stories.headline: Oil prices surge 8% as OPEC announces surprise production cut
stories.body: Crude oil prices jumped 8% after OPEC+ members agreed to slash production by 1.2 million barrels per day starting next month, catching markets off guard and threatening to reignite inflationary pressures.

stories.headline: China imposes sweeping new tech export controls on rare earth metals
stories.body: Beijing announced restrictions on exports of gallium and germanium, critical materials used in semiconductor manufacturing, in what analysts call an escalation of the US-China technology war.

stories.headline: Regional bank deposits plunge as contagion fears spread
stories.body: Several mid-tier US regional banks reported significant deposit outflows this quarter, echoing the crisis that toppled Silicon Valley Bank last year. The KBW Regional Banking Index fell 4.2%.

stories.headline: EU carbon border tax triggers retaliatory trade measures
stories.body: The European Union Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism has drawn sharp criticism from India and Brazil, who announced counter-tariffs on EU goods, raising fears of a global trade war over climate policy.

stories.headline: Treasury yields hit 16-year high as bond selloff accelerates
stories.body: The 10-year US Treasury yield surged past 5% for the first time since 2007, as investors dumped bonds amid concerns that the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer than expected.

stories.headline: Semiconductor shortage worsens amid Taiwan Strait tensions
stories.body: Global chip production faces new disruptions as military activity in the Taiwan Strait raises insurance costs for shipping routes, with TSMC warning of potential supply chain interruptions.

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank credit default swaps spike on contagion fears
stories.body: The cost of insuring Deutsche Bank debt against default surged to its highest level since the 2020 pandemic crash, reviving concerns about systemic risk in the European banking sector.

stories.headline: Federal Reserve Board members vote unanimously to hold rates at current levels
stories.body: In a closely watched decision, the Fed voted to keep the federal funds rate unchanged at 5.25-5.50%, citing persistent inflation risks while acknowledging recent signs of economic slowdown.

stories.headline: OPEC extends production cuts through end of year amid demand concerns
stories.body: The oil cartel announced an extension of its 1.2 million barrel per day production cut through December, driving Brent crude above $95 a barrel.

stories.headline: US-China chip war escalates as Washington bans AI chip exports
stories.body: The Commerce Department issued new rules blocking exports of advanced AI training chips to China, citing national security concerns.

stories.headline: European banking stocks tumble as Credit Suisse fears resurface
stories.body: Shares of major European banks fell sharply after reports that a major European lender is seeking emergency capital, reviving fears of a 2008-style banking crisis.

stories.headline: Global supply chains disrupted as Red Sea shipping attacks intensify
stories.body: Houthi attacks on commercial vessels in the Red Sea have forced major shipping companies to reroute around Africa, adding weeks to delivery times and pushing freight costs up 300%.

stories.headline: Bank of Japan unexpectedly raises interest rates for first time in 17 years
stories.body: In a historic move, the BOJ ended its negative interest rate policy, marking the end of the last negative rate regime worldwide.

stories.headline: US national debt surpasses $35 trillion as deficit spending accelerates
stories.body: The US fiscal deficit widened to $1.7 trillion for the year as debt servicing costs hit record levels, prompting rating agencies to place the sovereign rating on negative outlook.
